$comment: VENDORED COPY for the WS-A contract test in claude-copilot/tools/cc. Source of truth is copilot-control-tower/docs/01-architecture/schemas/resolve.schema.json — update this copy from there, not the reverse.
title: copilot resolve --explain --json
description: Per-item explanation of layered resolution. `live_hash_matches:false` => render MODIFIED, never a stale 'signed OK'. CONFIRMED 2026-07-07 against the upstream design-control-tower-integration.md §1.3, which describes the exact same per-item shape: `{ item, dimension, winning_layer, winning_sha, shadowed[], signer_of_introducing_commit, live_hash_matches (bool) }`. No divergence found; this file already matched.

items.$comment: cli-contract.md describes a per-item object shape but not the top-level container. Since every verb must carry a top-level schema_version, the per-item objects are wrapped in an `items` array under the envelope. Owner to confirm the container name.

product.description: Product attribution of the winning layer (e.g. 'claude', 'codex', 'knowledge', 'cli'). Config-driven — not a closed enum. Carried through from the layer's `product` field (see four-tier-topology.md §4) so output is groupable by product without re-deriving it from repo names. A layer belongs to exactly one product x tier.

winning_sha.description: Resolved SHA of the winning layer's content, sourced from the lockfile; null when no lockfile entry exists yet (e.g. before first `update`). Required-but-nullable, like signer_of_introducing_commit.

shadowed.$comment: Prose shows `shadowed[]` with an unspecified element shape; element schema left open (any) until the CLI freezes it.

signer_of_introducing_commit.description: Signer identity of the commit that introduced the winning content; null when unsigned/unknown. Security-relevant: an absent/null signer must not be rendered as trusted.
signer_of_introducing_commit.$comment: Fail-closed: consumer treats a missing/null signer as unsigned.

live_hash_matches.description: Whether the on-disk content still matches winning_sha. Security-critical: false => MODIFIED. A missing value is treated as not-matching (fail-closed); the CLI must always emit it.
